data.title: Japanese company SBI Holdings aims to acquire a majority stake in Coinhako, a crypto exchange in Singapore.
data.description: SBI Holdings of Japan has agreed to acquire a majority ownership stake in the Singapore-based cryptocurrency exchange, Coinhako. The agreement was formalized through a letter of intent.

data.title: Sivers Semiconductors launches new Ka-band SATCOM products, advancing phased array terminals.
data.description: Sivers Semiconductors AB, based in KISTA, Sweden, has introduced new Ka-band Beamformer ICs and Panels that enable advanced Phased Array SATCOM Terminals. The company, recognized for its expertise in photonics and wireless technologies, revealed the availability of its Cloudchaser beamforming technology on February 17, 2026. This development marks a significant leap in the industry, showcasing cutting-edge solutions for next-generation satellite communication systems.

data.title: Steak 'N Shake sales surged after accepting Bitcoin payments, funds staff bonuses. Nine months post-launch, same-store sales rose dramatically. Biglari Holdings-owned chain cites "transformative power" of Bitcoin.
data.description: Steak ‘n Shake, a fast food chain owned by Biglari Holdings (NYSE: BH), saw a significant increase in same-store sales after implementing Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) payments. The popular burger chain credited the "transformative power" of Bitcoin for driving this growth. According to the company, same-store sales have surged since the launch of Bitcoin payments nine months ago. By integrating Bitcoin into its operations, Steak ‘n Shake successfully combined its cash-producing business model with the innovative potential of cryptocurrency. This strategic move has led to a substantial boost in sales for the chain.

data.title: Elbit Systems secures $435M contracts for advanced defense systems from international customer.
data.description: Elbit Systems Ltd., a defense technology company based in Haifa, Israel, revealed on February 17, 2026, that it secured multiple contracts valued at around $435 million from a global client. The contracts entail the delivery of various solutions and services.

data.title: Trump says inflation down to 2.4%, markets up despite choppy action. Inflation at 8-month low, below 2.5% forecast.
data.description: President Donald Trump commemorated Presidents' Day by highlighting a cooling economy and historically low crime rates. Despite this, financial markets are displaying a significant contrast between soaring blue-chip stocks and a faltering tech industry. In a social media post on Monday, Trump asserted that "Prices and Inflation are Way Down," a statement backed by the latest data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics. The annual inflation rate dropped to 2.4% in January 2026 from 2.7% in December, surpassing economists' predictions of 2.5%. This marks the lowest inflation rate recorded since May 2025. Trump praised this decline, emphasizing that it signifies positive economic progress. However, the data reveals a mixed scenario. While energy costs decreased, with gasoline prices falling by 3.2% during the month, consumers experienced price hikes in other sectors such as a 6.5% increase in airline fares. data.title: Nasdaq falls by 50 points, records weekly loss, investor sentiment at 'Fear' level due to inflation report. Indices down by 1.2%-2.1%.
data.description: The Fear and Greed index by CNN Money indicated a decrease in market sentiment, remaining in the "Fear" zone on Friday. U.S. stocks had a mixed settlement, with the Nasdaq Composite dropping approximately 50 points post the inflation report. The annual inflation rate fell from 2.7% to 2.4% in January, below the expected 2.5%, marking the lowest figure since May 2025. Core inflation, excluding food and energy, also decreased from 2.7% to 2.5% year over year, hitting the lowest level since March 2021. Major indices experienced losses for the week - the S&P 500 declined by 1.4%, the Dow by 1.2%, and the Nasdaq by 2.1%. Applied Materials Inc. (NASDAQ: AMAT) saw an 8% increase in its stock after a successful quarterly report.

data.title: Inventiva released preliminary 2025 financial results on Feb 17, 2026, highlighting cash reserves, revenue for the year.
data.description: Daix in France and New York City, New York, USA, February 17, 2026 – Inventiva (IVA), a biopharmaceutical company listed on Euronext Paris and Nasdaq, disclosed preliminary unaudited financial figures for the year ended December 31, 2025. The Company highlighted key metrics such as cash reserves, cash equivalents, and revenues, pertaining to its focus on developing oral therapies for metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH).

data.title: Essity's Capital Markets Day on May 7, 2026 in Gothenburg, Sweden with President Ulrika Kolsrud & team. 09:00 - 14:00 CET.
data.description: Mark your calendars for May 7, 2026, when Essity will host its Capital Markets Day at its Gothenburg, Sweden office. Taking place from 09:00 to around 14:00 CET, the event will feature President and CEO Ulrika Kolsrud, along with key members of the Executive Management Team. This announcement was made in Stockholm on February 17, 2026.
